title: Chapter Seventy-Two: Rewards
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

How long had he been in pitch-black unconsciousness?

Sunlight struck first. White. Then the sensation of a deep cotton mass pushing his soul, and he was rising through layers of darkness toward waking.

Consciousness flashed like a beam of light. He instantly counted the time that had passed -- a habit of thought that had already become instinct.

From the Dragon-Phoenix Gathering to the Hwangbo family, back to South Zhili to save Won-chang, then the Blood Flame Sect headquarters before the Namgung bastards. Just the time spent moving was enormous. Significant incidents overlapping. The days spent were eighty-seven. Lifespan lost to sword song: fifteen days total.

*Always counting. Always the count.*

The bed was familiar. The softness unique to Radiant Demon Wing's quarters. The air's smell -- cozy, neat. It meant safety.

He observed internally. His entire body's blood paths, eight extraordinary vessels, twelve meridians, and three dantians. The strength and mobility had become very weak -- the recoil from changing the Blood Spirit Formation into demon-subduing formation key points. The burden his Hundred Meetings point placed on his upper dantian had grown larger too.

*Recuperation. Days of it.*

His stomach was empty but his body had not weakened too much. He guessed a few days of unconsciousness, no more.

"There's movement. Are you awake?"

The voice was thick with power, even when speaking gently. Inner power rippling without conscious effort. A very powerful supreme master who spoke without opening the door first, mindful of his nephew's privacy.

He knew immediately. Both the familiar voice and the man who had become mindful after learning of their blood relation.

"Yes, Squad Leader. Please come in."

"Alright."

The door opened with a creak. Ma Jin filled the frame -- large-built, muscles tightly woven enough to show through black clothes, scars on his face meaning hundreds of battles. The Radiant Demon Wing's leader who had made his name even more renowned while exterminating the Hwangbo main family.

Yeon-shin looked at his maternal uncle anew. He had thought many things right after being captured by the Seventh Apostle. He thought he might never see him again.

Ma Jin sat on the bed's edge. He opened his mouth while looking down intently at his lying nephew.

"How is your body? You must have fought the Hwangbo family head."

"How are *you*?"

"They said it was war so they attacked together. The family head's martial might was quite powerful -- used martial arts called secret techniques I'd never encountered. Joined hands with the Annihilation Team leader, and only after passing seventy exchanges did I take his head. Recovered from internal injuries during the return." A brief pause. "Your turn."

"True qi flows. Blood circulation is sound. I need meals and rest." He hesitated. "What day is it?"

"Five days before summer solstice."

The fifteenth of June. Half of his sixteenth year already spent. Three years and six months remained. About fifteen days had to be counted as lifespan drawn for sword song.

He sat up using his abdominal muscles and began stretching his legs. Ma Jin was pushed back inadvertently.

"What are you doing?"

"Lying down isn't recuperation. I need to feel sunlight. Sense the late spring breeze."

"When you're not even an elf --"

Yeon-shin stopped his uncle from following and left the inner quarters. He had to stop immediately outside. Senior disciples passing through the Radiant Demon Wing hall approached with bright faces.

"Lightning Genius is up."

"Are you alright?"

"I heard your activities were quite dramatic -- would you tell us about it?"

"More than that, is it true you were carried by the Sect Lord? How was the speed?"

Something he had not experienced in a long time. They were Radiant Demon Wing blue-ranked seniors who had shown interest every time their youngest completed a mission. Though Yeon-shin had escaped the lower ranks, in their eyes he still seemed like a young junior.

His goal was the Divine Sword Squad Leader's purple rank. Blue-ranked warriors were the core of Desolate Sect. He had to mind his reputation among them.

He walked with them to the path passing the great martial arts grounds, answering each question. More seniors gathered. Eventually they began talking among themselves.

"The main sect has certainly become turbulent. Including how the Sect Lord returned caring for Lightning Genius."

"Are there more incidents?"

"The entire murim is entering chaotic times. The seventeen squads' hands are very lacking, but the ambition of the families under the main sect has caught fire. Word spreads they've begun fighting over mission assignments. Your maternal family, the Desolate Ma family, is involved too."

Words spoken with the uninhibited manner of blue-ranked masters who could act as strong ones anywhere in the Central Plains. Their personalities were as magnanimous as their lofty martial might.

"There's more. The genius fellow -- the one locked in the prison. The Scorching Divine Meridian."

"If you mean the Hwangbo family's young master, that's right. There's much talk about his treatment."

"I don't understand why they don't kill him. Isn't the Hwangbo family's martial arts remaining itself a future trouble?"

The madman who had held a rose in his mouth. A different kind of madman from the Seventh Apostle.

*Compared to the conflicts between families, it's nothing special.*

"I'll tell you stories later," Yeon-shin said. "Let's have a sword discussion sometime."

He shook them off with kind words. He headed straight for the General Administration Office, setting aside recovery. He could not bear waiting for the merit settlement.

---

"Radiant Demon Wing's Jeong Yeon-shin. Is that correct?"

"Yes."

The clerk at the entry register checked his Desolate badge. A vivid golden tree pattern gilded on it. The characters for Lightning Genius engraved below. No longer just an alias -- it was the courtesy name officially bestowed by the Sect Lord during the coming-of-age ceremony.

The clerk returned the badge immediately. A formality. No one could fail to recognize the golden character "Desolate" meaning direct disciple of the Sect Lord.

The hall's interior smelled strongly of ink. Long stretched-out desks, very classical, and dozens of clerks each holding brushes and writing down characters. A vast office, no smaller than an ordinary martial arts ground.

He sent sound transmission to the one in the seat of honor.

*-Radiant Demon Wing's Lightning Genius. I wish to report my mission.*

A robust man in brown robes stood up. Middle-aged, thick black beard, and his reaction was very quick.

"This way please!"

He stretched out his hand while practically shouting. Very courteous. They said Desolate Sect's Grand Administrator could shout even at black ranks regarding budget and personnel. Could this booming, cheerful person be that man?

Yeon-shin paused. Gazes turned. Some clerks refocused on work while others whispered. His previous murim experience guided him. He had learned conduct while losing in sword discussions.

"I greet the many pillars of the main sect. Radiant Demon Wing's Lightning Genius."

He clasped his hands in respect to the General Administration Office clerks. Most warriors under heaven tended to keep their distance from those who recite writing. He could not be like that. He was young, and those holding personnel authority were like kings.

The silence was brief. Clerks who rose in numbers returned the courtesy. Their faces were strange. He was the Sect Lord's direct disciple -- a status originally held only by imperial family members. They had never received courtesy first from a master wearing the golden character.

*Good. Small seeds. Sprout a small goodwill.*

In a separate building, the two sat facing each other.

"I was thinking of serving dragon well tea, if that's alright."

"I will gratefully enjoy it."

The Grand Administrator called for tea and meanwhile Yeon-shin studied him. Eye level quite high matching his large build. Though head of the Administration Office, he had learned martial arts -- intense momentum touched Yeon-shin's skin. He did not seem like someone skilled at flattery.

The man pulled out a bundle of papers thick as a book. Dense writing.

"The Dragon-Phoenix Gathering and Pingyin County Hwangbo branch mission. Destroying Blood Flame Sect branches while roaming Shandong and South Zhili. Sweeping away blood demons before those Namgung bastards."

He looked up.

"It's been quite a while. There were many witnesses. Our side clearly observed the matters. White-ranked warrior Shin So-bin's report, letters from two branch officials, Blue Eyed Sword Demon, Bewitching Sword Demon. Even Zhongnan Sect's Sword Dragon sent word."

"Are you saying these matters have already been settled?"

"That's right. With one simple cross-check, you will receive rewards as a blue-ranked warrior."

The Grand Administrator raised the corners of his mouth. His smile was big.

"However -- the Blood Flame Sect headquarters incident... since the Sect Lord hasn't given word, we need a clear report. That will take time. So before that."

He paused. Opened his mouth again.

"Let's discuss the settled rewards. Fifty taels of silver, martial clothes made entirely of thousand-cocoon silk, the right to request martial arts instruction from the Divine Sword Squad Leader, the main sect's high-grade spirit medicine. Of course you may choose. If you specifically--"

"Spirit medicine."

No hesitation. The Grand Administrator's eyes grew slightly larger.

"Hmm? That's not what I meant."

"Pardon?"

"I was just asking about the spirit medicine -- whether you'll take it now or request storage at the Medicine Hall. The Medicine Hall master is an elf. They can keep the pills in optimal condition, free from humidity and temperature. I mentioned it because it's certainly trustworthy to leave it there."

"...It's difficult to understand your words."

The Grand Administrator laughed.

"Everything is yours, Young Hero. All that I mentioned is so."

"Isn't this excessive?"

"If it was just the Dragon-Phoenix Gathering, it would be so." His tone sharpened with admiration. "The merit of destroying the Hwangbo branch with just three blue ranks is great. I heard it was thanks to your unique martial arts. Minimizing the power used to strike the main family -- that ranks third."

Yeon-shin said nothing. He was unfamiliar with praise.

"This is interesting," the Grand Administrator said. "Shall we discuss the second? You alone destroyed six Blood Flame Sect branches in Xuzhou. Even a letter came from the Xuzhou government office, praising Desolate Sect as Ming's divine sword."

His expression was very satisfied.

"Securing influence over government offices is the main sect's key task. A blue rank alone earning praise from branch great masters? It's worth making the rewards large and widely known."

The man praised him openly. Yeon-shin's expression stiffened. Great praise approached as unfamiliar awkwardness.

"It was your first murim venture as a blue rank? Get used to it. As a main family blue-ranked master, as the Sect Lord's direct disciple -- even more so."

"...Thank you for the advice."

"Nothing special." He waved it off. "Among the settled merits, the Clear Night Valley incident in South Zhili ranks first. You burst into the war between the Namgung family and Blood Flame Sect. You are the main sect's legitimate guardian of the Great Ming Code."

WOONG.

Energy waves suddenly sprouted from the Grand Administrator's body. Mind and true qi rose simultaneously. Yeon-shin was greatly surprised.

*His cultivation is in the realm of Desolate Sect black ranks.*

"Those fundamentally lowborn mongrels watched idly?" His voice carried strong power. "The secular martial families see our main sect as thorns in their eyes. They wanted to kill without getting blood on their hands. A rising talent who would become Desolate Sect's precious sword."

Great anger formed on his expression. His face was frightening even speaking of past events.

Yeon-shin received a teacup from the servant. While bringing the cup to his mouth, he waited for the anger to subside. The dragon well tea was said to be precious, but even after several sips he could not feel anything special.

*Perhaps I'm far from the tea ceremony. A hobby that doesn't match my life.*

"I briefly showed discourtesy." The Grand Administrator bowed his head slightly. "The main sect gained a great justification. We will use the Namgung family as vanguard against the Thirteen Evil Sects."

"You mean seeking cooperation?"

"It means we gained justification to force them by martial might. We intend to deal with those who watched you idly according to murim's law."

Though he calmed his energy waves, his smile was chilling. Murim's law in this context was obvious. It meant gripping the Namgung family and hammering them like a battering ram against the Thirteen Evil Sects.

*Using the grudges of the Sect Lord's direct disciple as pretext.*

Yeon-shin felt the murim's winter. Severely cold. Even from allies.

"Borrowing a knife to kill, using poison to counter poison," the Grand Administrator said, as if finding it amusing. "They'll have nothing to say if they receive the same treatment."

"That would be part of the mission?"

"Rather than war -- life-and-death martial matches. We'll bind Namgung's body with justification and only strike their leadership. If you wish to volunteer, focus on recuperation. There might be competition in selecting volunteers -- martial competitions."

"Then regarding the Blood Flame Sect headquarters..."

"The day is late. Perhaps those rewards won't be something I can raise at my level." A gentle smile. A polite dismissal.

He listed the logistics -- silver and silk clothes sent to the Radiant Demon Wing hall, an order sealed with the Sect Lord's stamp for the Divine Sword Squad Leader upon her return. The spirit medicine stored at the Medicine Hall.

Then, at the door, a final word. The Grand Administrator's face wore an ambiguous smile.

"Speaking out of this old man's concern -- it seems better for you to keep your distance from the Desolate Ma family."

"Why..."

Yeon-shin stared at him. He spoke of someone else's maternal family. Discussed conduct. It was a different matter. A crossed line.

The Grand Administrator waved his hand.

"I'm hesitant you might misunderstand. These are words of goodwill. Since great prestigious families generally have divided factions... your recent murim journey has caused great waves there. It's awkward to say more since it's not my family matter."

"...Thank you for the advice."

Yeon-shin slightly bowed his head and clasped his hands. The Grand Administrator returned the courtesy, then scratched his head -- one of many appearances unbefitting his age, build, and martial might. Innate personality.

"Take care."

A broad smile saw him out.

---

On the way back.

Dusk already falling. Was this year's June closer to early summer? A streak of crimson sunlight hazily seeped into his sleeve. A sunset that dyed his heart turbulent.

*The Desolate Ma family keeps holding me back. Hearing from others to be careful of my maternal family.*

He pulled his robe tight. The wind brushing his cheek and stirring his collarbone was cold. Every time he completed a mission and returned, he had to mind his conduct. Unwelcome. Time was lacking even just focusing on merits and reputation.

*I clearly told grandfather. I said not to try wielding blood ties. Firmly declared it after winning the match with Ma Se-yeon. Yet my bloodline became known through the coming-of-age ceremony. The gazes changed. Not as a warrior from a countryside martial house -- as a noble rising star associated with the Desolate Ma family's movements.*

This must not be. Family matters were not limited to individuals. He might get entangled in unrelated grudges. Not much time remained. Troublesome matters had to be avoided early.

Just then, a good measure came to mind.

Therefore --

"Yeon-shin!"

The handsome youth running urgently was welcome. Familiar white-ranked warrior, lightness technique quite accomplished. The branch family relative who had lost to him twice. Could not be forgotten.

*Ma Se-in.*

Grandfather Ma Yeon-jeok's adopted grandson. The Desolate Ma family's successor. One of the culprits who had disturbed his mind. Here karma that heaven had casually thrown was tied.

Something big must have happened, for him to come on the day Yeon-shin regained consciousness. Perhaps his tactless uncle Ma Jin had told him.

"What's your business?"

"Just, let me catch... my breath..."

Three exhales. Ma Se-in regulated his breathing. He was one who overwhelmed Yeon-shin in inner power quantity alone -- his birth environment different, spirit medicine like rice -- but he had clearly run far.

"Speak now."

"The family elders want to see you."

"The elders?"

"The senior members and my mother."

"Let's go. It's fine to visit now?"

Surprise crossed his face. He had not imagined such ready agreement.

They walked together. Ma Se-in glanced at Yeon-shin's profile.

"I heard you received quite severe internal injuries."

"And?"

"Your energy waves are the same as before. How can that be when you're weakened? Have you grown that much stronger? In such a short time?"

"More importantly." Yeon-shin avoided answering. "This matter of calling me now. Does grandfather know about it?"

"...No. He went to the Sect Lord's office."

*So. Summoned while the patriarch is away. Zhu Lianting moves fast.*

The corners of Yeon-shin's mouth rose slightly.

"Your hard fist technique was impressive," he said casually. The Eternal Blossom Fist's advancing thunder move. "They say prestigious families' martial arts number dozens. Is the family members' martial arts different from yours?"

"Naturally so."

"Interesting."

That was the end of it. They walked in silence until they reached the hall where the Desolate Ma family leaders were staying. Ma Se-in gestured to the gatekeepers.

"It's here."

The main gate was very large. Comparable to the Radiant Demon Wing hall. Even though they were borrowing it, one could guess how lofty their authority was.

It was just before the end of the day.

---
